# Claude Code Gateway

> An open-source Python server that exposes the Claude Code CLI as an OpenAI-compatible REST API. Use your Claude Code subscription as an API endpoint for any AI tool.

## What It Does

- Wraps the Claude Code CLI as a `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint
- Compatible with 30+ AI tools that speak the OpenAI API format
- Requires only a Claude Code subscription (Max or Pro) ã no separate Anthropic API key
- Supports streaming (SSE) and non-streaming responses
- Handles session continuity for multi-turn conversations
- Automatic model mapping (GPT model names ã Claude equivalents)

## Key Files

- [README.md](README.md): Setup, configuration, compatible tools
- [src/main.py](src/main.py): FastAPI app entry point, CORS middleware, router registration
- [src/config.py](src/config.py): Configuration via `CCG_` environment variables (Pydantic Settings)
- [src/routes/chat_completions.py](src/routes/chat_completions.py): Main chat completions endpoint (streaming + blocking)
- [src/routes/models.py](src/routes/models.py): Model listing endpoint
- [src/routes/health.py](src/routes/health.py): Health check endpoint
- [src/services/claude_runner.py](src/services/claude_runner.py): Subprocess wrapper that invokes the Claude Code CLI
- [src/services/stream_adapter.py](src/services/stream_adapter.py): Converts Claude NDJSON output to OpenAI SSE format
- [src/utils/model_mapper.py](src/utils/model_mapper.py): Maps GPT model names and short aliases to Claude model IDs
- [src/utils/message_formatter.py](src/utils/message_formatter.py): Converts OpenAI message arrays to a single Claude prompt string
- [src/models/openai_types.py](src/models/openai_types.py): Pydantic models for OpenAI request/response schemas
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml): Docker deployment, mounts `~/.claude` credentials into the container
- [.env.example](.env.example): Template for all supported environment variables

## Compatible Tools

### Coding Agents
Cline, Aider, OpenHands, bolt.diy, Goose, Roo Code, SWE-agent, OpenAI Codex CLI

### Chat Interfaces
Open WebUI, LibreChat, Lobe Chat, AnythingLLM, Jan, ChatBot UI

### Agent Frameworks
LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, LlamaIndex, Pydantic AI, OpenAI Agents SDK, Smolagents

### IDE Extensions
Continue.dev, Tabby, Void, Zed

### Visual Builders
n8n, Dify, Flowise, Langflow

## Supported Models

- `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` ã Claude Sonnet 4 (default)
- `claude-opus-4-20250514` ã Claude Opus 4
- `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` ã Claude Haiku 4.5

GPT aliases are automatically remapped: `gpt-4` / `gpt-4o` / `gpt-4-turbo` ã Sonnet; `gpt-3.5-turbo` / `gpt-4o-mini` ã Haiku. Short aliases `sonnet`, `opus`, and `haiku` are also accepted. Native `claude-*` model IDs pass through unchanged.

## API Endpoints

- `POST /v1/chat/completions` ã Chat completions (streaming SSE + blocking JSON)
- `GET /v1/models` ã List available Claude models in OpenAI format
- `GET /health` ã Health check

## Configuration

All settings use the `CCG_` prefix and can be set in `.env` or as environment variables.

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `CCG_HOST` | `0.0.0.0` | Server bind address |
| `CCG_PORT` | `8080` | Server port |
| `CCG_DEFAULT_MODEL` | `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` | Default Claude model |
| `CCG_DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS` | `10` | Max conversation turns per request |
| `CCG_CLAUDE_CLI_TIMEOUT` | `300` | CLI subprocess timeout in seconds |
| `CCG_WORKING_DIR` | `` | Working directory for CLI (empty = temp dir) |

## Quick Start

1. Clone the repository
2. Copy `.env.example` to `.env`
3. Run with Docker: `docker compose up --build`
4. Point your AI tool's OpenAI base URL to `http://localhost:8080/v1`

Local (without Docker): install dependencies with `pip install -r requirements.txt`, then run `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080`.

## Architecture Notes

The gateway spawns the `claude` CLI as a subprocess for each request. The Docker image mounts `~/.claude` and `~/.claude.json` from the host so the container reuses the authenticated session from the host machine. Streaming responses are converted from Claude's NDJSON format to the OpenAI SSE (`data: {...}` lines) format by `stream_adapter.py`. Session continuity across turns is maintained by passing a `session_id` back to the client and accepting it on subsequent requests.
